statement.markdown: Given a string `s` and an integer `k`, write a function that checks if `s` can be rearranged to form a palindrome, where at most `k` swaps are allowed.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a string `s` and an integer `k`.
statement.outputFormat: Return `true` if `s` can be rearranged to form a palindrome, and `false` otherwise.
statement.notes: The character case should be ignored.

examples.explanation: Swap b and c to get palindrome
No way to form palindrome with at most 2 swaps

hints.text: First hint text: Use two pointers, one at the start and one at the end of the string.

editorial.title: Editorial title: Two Pointer Palindrome
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: We use two pointers, one at the start and one at the end of the string. We count the occurrences of each character in the string using a counter. We then calculate the minimum swaps needed to make the string a palindrome. If the minimum swaps needed is less than or equal to k, we return true; otherwise, we return false.

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, s: str, k: int) -> bool:
